lines.text: Monica, Chandler, and Phoebe's, Phoebe is at the sink and Chandler is looking at a ring brochure.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Pheebs, can you help me pick out an engagement ring for Monica? I can't figure this out! It's so hard! Should I get her a (turning to each page) Tiffany cut or a Princess cut or a-ah-ah! Paper cut!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Now, have you told anyone else?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, I don't want to tell anybody else because I don't want Monica to find out.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You told me.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, it's because I trust you, you're one of my best friends, and you walked in on me when I was looking at ring brochures.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah well, once again not knocking pays off. I only wish you hadn't been on the toilet.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Me too.
Joey and Ross enter causing Chandler to quickly hide the brochure behind his back.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey. (Heads straight for the fridge.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey! So Chandler, wanna go to the coffeehouse?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh all right.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, coffeehouse.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh perfect, we were just gonna see if you wanted to go.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh well, we don't because we got...the...other pl-place.
Joey returns with a piece of pizza as Chandler and Phoebe exit.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: How rude.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, I'm sorry. You wanna bite? (Holds his piece out for him.)
Opening Credits
Central Perk, Phoebe and Rachel are sitting on the couch.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So how are things going with Paul?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Good. Although y'know, he-he's a private guy. Y'know, I wish I could get him to open up a little bit, share some feelings.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That's easy! You just have to think of him as a-as a jar of pickles that won't open.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So what are you saying; I should run him under hot water and bang his head against a table?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No that's what you do when you want to get the truth out of someone.

lines.speaker: Paul
lines.text: (entering) Hi honey.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (To Rachel) Watch this.

lines.speaker: Paul
lines.text: (To Rachel) How are you?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (To Phoebe) Okay.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hi Paul!

lines.speaker: Paul
lines.text: Hi Phoebe.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So how are things going with you?

lines.speaker: Paul
lines.text: Can't complain.
Phoebe turns to Rachel and mimes remove a lid of a jar. Ross enters and Paul motions for Rachel to leave with him now.

lines.speaker: Paul
lines.text: (whispering) Come on.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay. (Gets up and starts to leave with Paul.)

lines.speaker: Paul
lines.text: (To Ross) Hey!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi!
They shake hands and their lines overlap.

lines.speaker: Paul
lines.text: Ross!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Great to see you!

lines.speaker: Paul
lines.text: Good to see you too!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: How you doing?

lines.speaker: Paul
lines.text: Good. Bye! (Starts to leave.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay! You take care!
Ross turns his back on Paul and makes a 'I hate that guy' face. Paul does the same thing.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey Pheebs, what-what was the deal with you and Chandler blowing us off before?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah! That was so weird, huh?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Phoebe, why'd you do it?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I didn't do it! It was Chandler! He's... He's mad at you!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?! Why?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Please, I think you know why.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I can't think of anything.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Come on Ross, you're a paleontologist, dig a little deeper.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wait a minute, is it because Joey and I didn't invite him to that Knicks game a couple of weeks ago?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Do you think that's something that he'd be mad at you for?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I guess it could.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well then I think that's it.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, if he's angry, he really shouldn't just cover it up. I-I wish he would just tell me the truth.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, if that's what you want you then you really should run his head under hot water and bang his head against a table.
Joey and Rachel's, Paul and Rachel are getting ready to go out. Paul is entering from the bathroom.

lines.speaker: Paul
lines.text: Honey I made a reservation at China Garden, is that okay?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah that's great. But first, wait, talk to me, talk to me. Tell me about your day.

lines.speaker: Paul
lines.text: It was fine.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay. Hey, what are you thinking? What are you thinking right now?

lines.speaker: Paul
lines.text: I'm thinking that you are looking really fine it that dress.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah that's great Paul, but y'know I wanna know what-(Puts her hands on his shoulders)-Wow, those are really great! I just wanna know what, what is behind this-this strong, silent exterior. Y'know they say that still waters run deep and I wanna swim in yours.

lines.speaker: Paul
lines.text: Are you talking about having sex?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No Paul, I don't know anything about you! Y'know, like-like your childhood! Tell me about your childhood!

lines.speaker: Paul
lines.text: Normal.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, well then how about puberty! Come on, that's always a painful time! Y'know your friends invite you to a slumber party and then they stick your hand in warm water while you're sleeping so that you pee in your sleeping bag.

lines.speaker: Paul
lines.text: Nope! That never happened to me!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, you're lucky you never met that bitch Sharon Majesky. Anyway, umm... The rest of you life, y'know? Any regrets?

lines.speaker: Paul
lines.text: Nope.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: All right Paul, I'm not asking for a lot here. Okay? Just give me something. Anything!

lines.speaker: Paul
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Paul
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: All right.

lines.speaker: Paul
lines.text: When I was six years old.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hm-mmm.

lines.speaker: Paul
lines.text: I wanted a big wheel. And instead my parents got me this little plastic chicken that you hop around on. It was embarrassing; kids made fun of me. That was a pretty tough year.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: That's-that's great! See? I already feel like I know you a little better! Thank you. Okay, come on. Now we can go eat. Let's go. (Gets up to leave, but Paul doesn't move.)

lines.speaker: Paul
lines.text: It was horrible. They called me chicken boy.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh!
[Time Lapse, Paul is now weeping uncontrollably in Rachel's arms.]

lines.speaker: Paul
lines.text: And in fifth grade I got into a fight. Well, it wasn't really a fight. Richard Darinvel bit me on the nose and, and I feel down. I still have a little scare right here (points to it) you can see it.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah. Yeah, I-I-I see the scare. Listen, Paul, I think this is really great that-that y'know, you shared your feelings. It's really, it's beautiful, but umm, what do you say we go share some food?

lines.speaker: Paul
lines.text: Oh, I couldn't eat now.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?! Wait! What are you talking about?! You love their Kung Pao Chicken!

lines.speaker: Paul
lines.text: Chicken? (Pointing to himself.) Chicken boy!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: My God, I'm sorry! I'm sorry! I didn't mean to do that! I wouldn't do that!
Paul screams like a chicken and breaks down into tears again.
A Jewelry Shop, Chandler and Phoebe are looking at engagement rings.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Nothin! This is the nine millionth ring store we've been too and I can't find the perfect ring! (Goes over to another display counter and starts pointing at rings.) Ugly ring! Ugly ring! Ugly ring! (Notices that one of the jewelers is watching him.) It's a beautiful selection. (The jeweler walks away slowly.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, so maybe you don't get her a ring. Maybe you-maybe you do something different. Y'know? Maybe you get her an engagement bracelet, y'know? Or an engagement tiara? Or-ooh! An engagement Revolutionary War musket! (Picks one up from the display in the corner.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Y'know, I'm so glad I picked you to help me with this.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Huh? Can you just imagine getting down on one knee and handing her this gorgeous piece of weaponry?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, I'm gonna stick with the ring. (Goes to another display counter.) Oh, this one's nice! (Pointing to another ring.) I like this one! Sir? Uh, kind sir? Can I see this one?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (coming over after returning the musket) Wait a minute, no, this is, this is the reason you brought me. Okay? I know how to haggle. So let me handle this from here on out.

lines.speaker: Male Jeweler
lines.text: Can I help you?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Uh-uh, yes. I would like to see that ring please.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Or not, whatever.

lines.speaker: Male Jeweler
lines.text: This ring is from the 1920s, it's a one and a half carat diamond with sapphires on either side.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Sir, can I ask you to umm, could you...hold out that ring and ask me to marry you?

lines.speaker: Male Jeweler
lines.text: Okay. (Holds out the ring, deadpan.) Will you marry me?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (choking up) Oh my God that's it, that's the ring! How much is it?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Chandler, I-I will handle this! (To the jeweler) How much is it?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: We will give you $10.

lines.speaker: Male Jeweler
lines.text: (angrily) Are you interested in this ring?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes! Yes, but I can only pay $8,000.

lines.speaker: Male Jeweler
lines.text: Okay, I can let it go at eight.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: We stand firm at $10.

lines.speaker: Male Jeweler
lines.text: (ignoring her) How would you like to pay?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Uh, credit card. (Reaches for it then realizes) Oh no! No-no, but I left my credit card with Joey. (To Phoebe) Okay, I'll go get it. You guard the ring.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay. (To the jeweler) Listen, I'm sorry about before. Do you have anything her for $10.

lines.speaker: Male Jeweler
lines.text: Uh yes, I have these two rather beautiful $5 bills. (Holds them up from his pocket.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I'll give you $1 for them.
Central Perk, Ross and Joey are there.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, remember how Chandler and Phoebe blew us off yesterday?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Remember? You-you were eating pizza.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay. Well, apparently Chandler's angry at us for not getting him a ticket to that Knicks game a couple of weeks ago.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, we're supposed to just get him a ticket?! That guy is always mooching off of us!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah! Anyway, I-I still think we should try to patch things up, y'know? Like uh, maybe we could get him to get tickets to another Knicks game and invite him.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh wow that's a great idea! And I still have his credit card.

lines.speaker: Gunther
lines.text: (handing them the bill) Here you go.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh. (Starts to get his money)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey-hey-hey-ho-ho, I got this one. Here you go. (Hands Gunther Chandler's card.) Y'know I gotta tell ya, sometimes I just-I don't get Chandler. Y'know, me and him do stuff all the time without you and you don't get all upset.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: All the time?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All the time!
Monica, Chandler, and Phoebe's, Monica is there as Rachel enters.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my God! Oh my God!!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Still crying?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Like a little girl. I know. I know. I know. This is all my fault; I wanted him to open up. But God, I didn't know that I was gonna unleash this-this weepy, clingy, moist monster!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Y'know, I only know of two surefire ways to shut a man up. And one of them is sex.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What's the other one?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I don't know, I've never had to use the other one. I'm just saying y'know, if we're having sex, he's not gonna be talking.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh that's right. You're the talker. (They both reflect on that briefly) Anyway uh, great idea! Umm, I gotta go to the store; I told him that I would buy him some more tissues.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, we have some...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No you don't!
The Jewelry Store, Phoebe is busy trying on virtually everything in the store. She's got earrings, rings, bracelets, and enough necklaces to put Mr. T to shame on.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay umm, I'd also like to try on the tiara. (The male jeweler hands it to her.) Oh yeah. Okay. (Puts it on.) (To the jeweler) What do you think, too much?

lines.speaker: Male Jeweler
lines.text: A tad.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay. Then, take the tiara back and let me hold the musket again. (He hands it to her and Phoebe poses in front of one of those small mirrors.) Something's missing. It's not... Okay oh, let me see the ring my friend picked out.

lines.speaker: Male Jeweler
lines.text: (to the female jeweler) Where's the 1920s princess cut ring.

lines.speaker: Female Jeweler
lines.text: I just sold it to that gentleman. (Points to the one walking out the store.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh my God!! (She runs after him, but sets of the security system, which locks the store's door and brings down a set of bars behind her, caging her in.) No! What?! Help me! Let me out! Now! (Points the musket at them.)
Commercial Break
Joey and Rachel's, Paul is still crying as Chandler enters.

lines.speaker: Paul
lines.text: Rachel?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No. How are ya Paul? (Starts to look for his credit card.)

lines.speaker: Paul
lines.text: (acting manly to try and cover up his crying) Okay. Chandler, did your dad ever hug you?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, did he hug you?!

lines.speaker: Paul
lines.text: No! No! It's just that, my dad never did. I miss my dad.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, you can see my dad in Vegas kissing other dads.

lines.speaker: Paul
lines.text: Hey Chandler?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Paul
lines.text: Would you.......Would you hug me?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'm a little busy here Paul.

lines.speaker: Paul
lines.text: That's exactly what my dad used to say! (Starts to breakdown again.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, a quick one. Come on hug it out. (Paul rushes over and hugs Chandler tightly.) Oh hey! There you go. (The hug continues.) Okay. (The hug continues.)

lines.speaker: Paul
lines.text: Five more seconds.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay! (Pushes him away.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (entering) Hey!

lines.speaker: Paul
lines.text: Joey! (Goes over and hugs Joey and picks him off of his feet.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Whoa-whoa-hey-hey! (Motions to Chandler, "What's going on?") Hi, Paul is it? (Paul nods yes and still hugging Joey.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Do you have my credit card?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yes, it's in my... In...in my pocket. (Paul hasn't dropped him yet.) (Chandler starts to reach for his front pocket.) My back pocket! My back pocket!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Thank God! (Grabs his card.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh hey listen I got us tickets to a Knicks game tonight.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, I can't go.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Come on! It'll be fun! Me, you, and Ross, and... Paul probably...
The Jewelry Store, Chandler is entering with his credit card and Phoebe is holding out another ring for him.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Chandler, I found the perfect ring. (Holding it out for her.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, that's uh, that's pretty nice but I'm gonna go with the one I picked first.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh my God Chandler, the one you picked is gone. It's over!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Some guy bought it. I'm sorry. I tired to stop it but they (points to the jeweler) put me in jail!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: They put you in jail?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: The little jail between the doors!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Phoebe, I asked you to guard the ring!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I know, I'm sorry! But y'know, this ring is better! Monica never even saw the other ring.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah but when he proposed to me with the ring I got goose bumps.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Maybe it was the guy.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It was the ring!
Central Perk, Ross is on the couch and Joey is entering.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey! So uh, was he excited about the tickets?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No! He blew us off!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I know!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I can't believe it. (Pause) Can I tell you something? I'm a little mad at him now.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Can I tell you something? Me too.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Y'know what? He didn't want to talk to us about being angry, well maybe we don't talk to him at all!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ooooh! Freeze him out.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That's right!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I like it!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Eh? We'll show him!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: From now on, it's gonna be Joey and Ross, best friends. (They shake hands.) Okay! We're gonna be the new Joey and Chandler.
Joey and Rachel's, Paul is writing something as Rachel enters.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi. I'm back.

lines.speaker: Paul
lines.text: Hey! I have so much more to tell ya, I've written it all down!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ah that's great. No actually that's... (In a sexy voice) That's great! That's really great! Y'know, I gotta tell ya writing, I mean writing, gets me uh, gets me kinda hot.

lines.speaker: Paul
lines.text: Wait! Wait! Listen! Listen to this! (Flips a couple pages and points to something.) Y'know what I wanted to be when I was that age?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: A lover?

lines.speaker: Paul
lines.text: A surfer.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh yeah surfer?

lines.speaker: Paul
lines.text: I wanted to be one with the waves, y'know?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, hold on real quick, hold on a second let me just uh, (sits on the counter and buttons her sweater to show some cleavage) get a little more comfortable here. Wait, now wait a second, this isn't too revealing is it?

lines.speaker: Paul
lines.text: (barely glancing at her) No. What ever happened to that little dude. (Pause) So full of dreams...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I don't care about the little dude! I can't! I cannot listen to anymore of this! Y'know, the only person who would want to listen to this is a mental health professional! And then it's only because they get paid $100 an hour! Do you know how much money I could've made listening to you? $2,000! And do you know when I figured that out? While you were talking!

lines.speaker: Paul
lines.text: What?! I can't believe you're trying to stifle me! When just 14 hours ago we figured out that that is exactly what my mother was trying to do to me!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh... I'm sorry. I... I-I don't mean-I didn't mean to stifle you. I... This is all just a little overwhelming.

lines.speaker: Paul
lines.text: Oh Rachel, I'm so sorry. I didn't mean to overwhelm you. It's just that, when those gates open, you... (Starts to breakdown.) Hard to close 'em. But they are closed now. Believe me.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I'm so glad, I'm so glad you shared. (Feeling his shoulder.) And I'm glad that you're done. What do you say we umm... (Nods in the direction of the bedroom.)

lines.speaker: Paul
lines.text: I would really like that. (They kiss.)
Rachel's Bedroom, Paul and Rachel are recovering.

lines.speaker: Paul
lines.text: That was...so good. (Starts crying again.)
Monica, Chandler, and Phoebe's, Chandler is looking at the ring he bought and not liking it.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (entering from her room) Hey.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I can't believe I let you talk me into buying this stupid gumball machine looking ring!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: It's not a stupid gumball machine looking ring! It's a beautiful ring!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, it's not! When I looked at the other ring I could see Monica's face when I gave it to her, y'know? And I could see her saying yes. When I look at this ring, all I see is a ring! Unless I look at it really closely and then I can see my own eye. (Does so and laughs.) Look, this is the most important thing I'm gonna do in my life. I wanna make sure it's perfect.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay. There may be a way that we can get the other ring back. 'Cause I heard the guy tell the jeweler where he was going to propose. So maybe we can get him to trade rings or something.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I can't do that.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well you certainly can't give her that stupid gumball ring.
A Restaurant, Chandler and Phoebe are entering. This is the place where the guy who bought Chandler's ring is going to propose.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: There he is! (Points.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay and he hasn't proposed yet because she has no ring on her finger.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wow! You're good! After this, we should solve crimes.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah! Okay, go, go, go get him.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, okay. (Walks over to him.) Excuse me sir? Could you come with me please? You have a phone call.

lines.speaker: Customer
lines.text: Who is it?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: It is your office.

lines.speaker: Customer
lines.text: Do you know who at my office?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: John?

lines.speaker: Customer
lines.text: Oh John! Great!
She brings him over to Chandler.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (To Chandler) Here he is.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (to him) Hi! Hi. Okay, there was a slight mix-up at the jewelry store, the ring you're about to propose with was supposed to be held for me. So, I'm gonna need to have that back. (The guy isn't sure.) But, in exchange I'm willing to trade you this beautiful, more expensive ring. (Looking at the ring.) Ew.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wow! I would trade.

lines.speaker: Customer
lines.text: It is beautiful, but I'm gonna use this one. Now, if you'll excuse me.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No-no! This is my fiancee (Phoebe) and her heart was set on that ring. You don't want to break her heart now do you?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, do you want to break a dying woman's heart?

lines.speaker: Customer
lines.text: You're dying?!
Phoebe coughs.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, she's dying... Of a cough apparently.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yes, and it is my dying wish to have that ring. See, if I'm not buried with that ring then my spirit is going to wander the nether world for all eternity...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (interrupting her) Okay, that's enough honey!

lines.speaker: Customer
lines.text: I don't know. (Pause) Let me see the ring.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Great! Okay, here. (Holds the ring up for him.)

lines.speaker: Customer
lines.text: (looks at it) All right. (Exchanges rings.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Thank you. Thank you. Thank you! (To his girlfriend) And you are about to marry a wonderful man! (She stunned and he's horrified.) Hey! I'm marrying a dead woman!
Joey and Rachel's, Joey and Ross are watching TV as Chandler enters.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Guys? (They ignore him.) I've got something important to tell ya. (Still nothing so he walks over and stands in front of the TV.) Guys? (They lean over to try and watch the TV, Chandler mimics them.) Guys?! (Pause) I'm gonna ask Monica to marry me.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (To Ross) I think we gotta end the freeze out.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wait a minute, is this, is this for real?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, check out the ring. (Shows it to them.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh my God!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So you two are really serious?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yep, pretty much.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You-you're gonna get married?! I mean... We're gonna be brothers-in-law! (They hug.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: And-and-and-and-and-and, and we're gonna be friends again!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (goes to hug him and stops short) Heyyyy-What?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh it's water under the bridge, forget it!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay! (They hug.) I was gonna wait 'til uh, it was official y'know? But I got so excited I just had to tell you guys because you're my best friends.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I think I'm gonna cry!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (who has just entered) Ugh! No more crying! Please! I just dumped one cry baby, I'll dump you too!
Ross and Joey urge Chandler to tell Rachel.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'm gonna ask Monica to marry me.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my God! Oh my God! Oh Chandler!! (Hugs him.) You guys are gonna be so happy!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I know.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (holding an empty tissue box) Where's all the tissues?! (Throws the box down in disgust.)
Ending Credits
Joey and Rachel's, continued from earlier. Chandler is showing Rachel the ring.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Check out the ring.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (gasps) Nice! One and a half carat easy.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (entering) Hi.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey-hey Pheebs!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Chandler's gonna ask Monica to marry him!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh I know, I helped pick out the ring.
Chandler laughs, turns, and sees that Ross and Joey aren't happy.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You told her before you told us?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, she walked in when I was looking at the ring brochures. You can understand that, right? (Ross and Joey look at each other and go back to watching the game on TV.) Guys? Guys? (Walks in front of them again.)
End
